_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved. Pool shell excavation is high-risk construction work under Reg 5.1.5 — this template is critical to get right.

hazards.title: Trench / excavation collapse
hazards.risk: Worker buried by collapsing excavation face; pool shell typically 1.5–2.5m deep, deeper for spa/wet deck

controls.title: Excavation > 1.5m: shoring (shielding/boxing) OR battering to safe angle for confirmed soil type (engineer-confirmed where uncertain)
Daily inspection before work and after any rain; stop work if soil conditions change
Spoil and machinery kept clear of zone of influence (min 1000mm from edge)

hazards.title: Mobile plant — excavator struck-by / crush
hazards.risk: Worker on foot struck by excavator or in swing radius

controls.title: 3m exclusion zone around operating excavator, marked with bunting/cones
Dedicated spotter at all times during excavation; clear hand signals or radio with operator
Operator HRWL verified at induction; valid for size of machine
No approach to machine until operator acknowledges, bucket grounded, attachments safe

hazards.title: Strike of underground service
hazards.risk: Excavator damaging gas / water / electrical / sewer / pool plumbing

controls.title: BYDA search current within 30 days; printed plans on site
Hand-dig / pothole within 500mm of any identified service before machine excavation
Services isolated where excavation crosses them (utility provider confirmation)

hazards.title: Contact with overhead power lines
hazards.risk: Excavator boom contacting energised overhead lines (6.4m exclusion under ESV)

controls.title: Where feasible, position machine and access so boom arc cannot reach overhead lines
Electrical spotter if any possibility of breach of 6.4m exclusion; asset owner approval if breach unavoidable
Tiger tails on overhead lines where exclusion zone routinely approached (asset owner installs)

hazards.title: Falls into excavation
hazards.risk: Worker, owner, or third party falling into open excavation; depth typically > 1.5m

controls.title: Guardrails or solid covers at end of each working day
Site perimeter fence locked outside working hours

hazards.title: Silica dust (rock / hard soil)
hazards.risk: Respirable crystalline silica from rock-breaking or dry-cutting in shell prep

controls.title: Water suppression on hydraulic breakers / rock-cutters
P2 respirator at minimum; P3 for sustained dry cutting

hazards.title: Noise > 85 dB(A)
hazards.risk: Hearing damage from excavator engine + rock breaker

controls.title: Class 5 ear muffs within 5m of operating plant

hazards.title: Vibration impact on adjacent structures
hazards.risk: Excavation close to dwelling slab, neighbouring boundary; vibration causing cracking, undermining footings

controls.title: Pre-condition photograph survey of adjacent structures and boundaries
Vibration monitoring where excavation within 3m of dwelling slab or boundary wall

tasks: Pre-start toolbox: walk site with operator and crew; confirm exclusion zone, services located, soil type, depth target, spoil location.
Confirm BYDA current; plans on site; services isolated where crossing dig.
Confirm overhead exclusion (6.4m) clear or asset owner controls in place.
Erect 3m exclusion bunting/cones around excavator working area.
Operator pre-start check: ROPs/FOPs, hydraulics, attachments, quick-hitch safety pin, reversing alarm, lights/horn.
Spotter takes position before machine starts; agreed hand signals.
Excavate top 300mm slowly; visually confirm no shallow services before continuing.
Continue in lifts (≤ 500mm); maintain shape per setout / engineering drawings.
Position spoil ≥ 1000mm from excavation edge OR load directly to truck for off-site disposal.
At 1.5m depth: install shoring OR confirm batter angle for soil (engineer-approved where uncertain).
Continuously monitor trench wall integrity; stop work if any sloughing, fissure, or change in soil/moisture.
No worker in excavation while machine operating overhead; no worker in zone of influence of spoil pile.
Hand-trim final shell shape per drawings.
End-of-day: edge protection or covers; perimeter fence locked.
Final inspection by site supervisor + sign-off in Hub before reinforcement commences.
